name: Ralph Burns
description: American jazz pianist, composer, and arranger (1922-2001)
abstract: Ralph Joseph P. Burns (June 29, 1922 – November 21, 2001) was an American jazz pianist, composer, and arranger.

works.t: I Don't Stand a Ghost of a Chance With You
Ziegfeld Follies of 1934: What is there to say?
Interlude in C (Based on Piano Concerto No. 2 in C Minor, Op. 18)
These Are the Things I Love
I Found a Million Dollar Baby

concerts.label: Woody Herman and His Thundering Herd: 40th Anniversary Concert

about_quotes.speaker: Chubby Jackson
about_quotes.evidence: "By mid-year the brilliant rhythm section of pianist Ralph Burns, bassist Chubby Jackson, guitarist Billy Bauer and drummer Dave Tough was in place."
about_quotes.doc_title: Chubby Jackson interviewed by Monk Rowe, San Diego, California, February 14, 1998

about_quotes.speaker: Woody Herman
about_quotes.evidence: Ralph had the great ability of writing for these odd instrumentations... and making it happen, especially against the big band.

about_quotes.speaker: Red Norvo
about_quotes.evidence: I'd had a band for a short while with Flip Phillips and Ralph Burns... Flip and Ralph Burns had worked with me in a small band.

about_quotes.speaker: Konitz Lee
about_quotes.evidence: That’s in fifty-one, right? That Free Forms album that Ralph Burns did that you played on? ... Yeah.
about_quotes.doc_title: Konitz Lee — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Charlie Barnet
about_quotes.evidence: Frances joined the band -- she was there when Ralph Burns was with the band. ... I had both of them.

about_quotes.speaker: John Pizzarelli, Jr.
about_quotes.evidence: We’ll get Johnny Mandel, we’ll get Ralph Burns... They spent $250,000... And there’s Clare Fischer, and there’s John Clayton, there’s Patrick Williams, there’s Johnny Mandel, there’s Michel Legrand...
about_quotes.doc_title: John Pizzarelli, Jr. interviewed by Monk Rowe, New York City, New York, January 29, 2000 [interview 1 of 2]
